title: PCR-based assay for genotyping of the slick mutation in cattle
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"akk2e","text":"Several mutations exist in the prolactin receptor gene (PRLR) of cattle that cause a short, sleek hair","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cq0vo","text":"coat.. The mutation is dominant so slick-haired cattle can have either one or","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dk1jh","text":"two copies of the mutation. The assay described here detects the most-well studied mutation (SLICK1)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"665pg","text":"that causes translation of a truncated PRLR of 461 amino acids. Originally described in Senepol cattle,","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8a7dm","text":"this mutation has also been introduced into Holsteins in Puerto Rico, Florida and elsewhere. Note that","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4uktn","text":"the assay will not detect other mutations in PRLR that also cause the slick phenotype.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
creator: Peter J Hansen
